ogTitle: Sturt vs Sultana Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sturt and Sultana Point.
heading: Sturt vs Sultana Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sturt, South Australia and Sultana Point, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sturt has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Sultana Point (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sturt or Sultana Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sturt has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Sultana Point scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sturt scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Sultana Point scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sturt or Sultana Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sturt has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
